explanation: Body is 337 words — exceeds the rubric's 300-word hard ceiling. Under v2 the underlying problem is also a smart-brevity issue: the Claude Code 2.1.x section packs ~14 features into a single comma-separated paragraph (vim visual mode, /usage, custom themes, --from-pr, native binaries, sandbox.network.deniedDomains, PID isolation, ultrareview, push-notification, MCP OAuth fixes, alwaysLoad). Bulleting that section would both bring the body under 300 words and improve scannability. Section leads are otherwise release-notes voice; weighting is fine.

criteria.name: Word count is between 80 and 300 words
criteria.evidence: Body is 337 words; the 300-word hard ceiling is exceeded.

criteria.name: A sentence with four or more comma-separated items should be bullets
criteria.evidence: The Claude Code 2.1.x section packs roughly 14 features into a single comma-separated paragraph that should be a bullet list.
